anchor_businesses.name: Tidewarden Cathedral — Velmonde
anchor_businesses.description: The Tidewarden Church's second most significant cathedral on Arneth (the primary is in the Shattered Reach Anchorage). Three hundred years old in its current form, built on foundations two hundred years older. Owns approximately 15% of coastal property. The banking office processes significant financial instruments. The library holds three hundred years of Church records and has recently had a section cleared without explanation.

charitable_stores.current_level: Significant — distributes meals daily from the Dock Quarter side entrance

resources.banking_instruments: Holds financial instruments across the coast's major property and commercial holdings
resources.library: Three hundred years of records. Section cleared — forty years of Church property records missing.

services: Daily charitable meals — Dock Quarter entrance, no conditions
Banking and financial instruments — fee-based, significant institutional weight
Library access — approved scholars, application required
Liturgical services — daily, open to public
hooks: The cleared library records
The Halvaine/Morne dispute — visible in Cathedral scheduling
The banking officer Aldeth Halvaine and her twenty-year anomaly
Pelvan's Tidewarden advisor providing incomplete information

anchor_businesses.name: Guild of the Sealed Ledger — Velmonde Office
anchor_businesses.description: The Ledger's most significant southern office. Holds debt records on half the coast. The senior notary Halvard Keld has been here for twenty years. The institutional neutrality is real and is the Ledger's core value — betraying it would end the institution's function. Currently carrying three specific pieces of information that institutional neutrality prevents sharing.

resources.records: Twenty years of coastal financial instruments, property records, debt holdings
resources.amber_point_instruments: Both the 80-year-old sale and the 40-year-old reclamation — plus Halvard Keld's knowledge of the reversionary clause

services: Notarial services — contracts, property instruments, debt recording
Employment — junior ledger keeper position currently open
hooks: The Amber Point reversionary clause — Halvard Keld knows and carries it alone
Neva Drenfield's manifest cross-referencing — visible to someone with access
The Halverness Factors thread — appears in Ledger records
Junior ledger keeper position — access to financial instruments across the coast

anchor_businesses.name: Factorate of the Coast — Administrative Building
anchor_businesses.description: The administrative engine of the Solvanic Dominion's coastal governance. Well-staffed, functionally effective, currently managing several situations at once with uneven visibility. The Factor-General's office is on the third floor. The notice board with official postings is in the ground floor entrance.

resources.administrative_records: Comprehensive records of coast governance, trade licensing, harbor operations
resources.disease_report: The Salthaven physician's report is in a locked drawer in the Factor-General's office. Secretary Vaela Dawnmere has a copy.

hooks: The Salthaven disease report — suppressed but visible to the right access
Unofficial security work through the Factor-General's personal aide
Succession preparation documents — locked drawer
Prince Pelvan's official schedule — Fenwick Saltfield manages it

anchor_businesses.name: The Warden's Rest
anchor_businesses.description: The best inn in Velmonde. Prince Pelvan has a formal dinner here tonight in the private room. The common room is the most information-rich social space in the Cathedral Quarter.

water.source: Cathedral Quarter well — reliable, fee included in room rate

food.source: Goldwater olive oil and wine direct supplier — the best table on the coast

pricing.wine_per_cup: Three iron chits (local) to one bronze mark (premium Goldwater vintage)

hooks: Pelvan's dinner in the private room — the spawn hook
The three faction advisors all stay here when in Velmonde
The Factor-General meets informal contacts here

anchor_businesses.name: Drenfield Shipping — Velmonde Office
anchor_businesses.description: The Velmonde face of Salthaven's most successful cargo operation. Neva Drenfield runs it from a well-organized office. The Solvanic naval assessment document that shouldn't be here is in a locked drawer.

resources.shipping_records: Three years of manifests, routes, cargo declarations
resources.naval_assessment: The incorrectly included Solvanic military document — locked drawer

services: Cargo shipping contracts — coast and regional
hooks: Neva's manifest cross-referencing and the Corsair payment thread
The incorrectly included naval assessment
The Halverness thread visible in three years of manifests

anchor_businesses.name: Bren Ashwick's School
anchor_businesses.location: Velmonde — Dock Quarter, three rooms above Fen Ashwick's sailmaker's workshop
anchor_businesses.description: Twenty-two children. Almost no budget. A teacher who has been doing this on stubbornness and community goodwill for three years. The most important building in the Dock Quarter and the only one that has no official status.

resources.budget: Near zero — some parent payments in goods, a Tidewarden charitable allocation barely covering lamp oil
resources.space: Three rooms, second floor, above the sailmaker's workshop. The sailmaker's rent increases have been creeping up for two years.

anchor_businesses.the_rent_problem: Currently at the edge of what Bren can manage. One more increase ends the school. The sailmaker has received a Merchant Tier buyout offer for the whole building.
hooks: Prince Pelvan has been visiting four times — the spawn hook
The rent problem — a concrete, solvable problem that earns Bren Ashwick's trust
The Pelvan thread comes through here
Community trust in the Dock Quarter is built through this location

velmonde_guaranteed: Salt Silence perfume stall (Foreign Quarter sub-section)
Three premium inns — Cathedral Quarter and Merchant Tier
Two budget lodging establishments — Dock Quarter
Main market — permanent stalls, 300 standard capacity
Wine merchants x4 — Goldwater wine primary
Physician — Cathedral Quarter (the one the Factor-General sent to Salthaven)

velmonde_roll_1d6.3: Music teacher — Cathedral Quarter, wealthy families
velmonde_roll_1d6.5: Rare book dealer — Archive-adjacent, Merchant Tier
velmonde_roll_1d6.6: Instrument maker — navigational instruments, Tidewatch clients

salthaven_guaranteed: Salthaven harbor — working fishing and cargo facility
Three alehouses — Drowned Compact community spaces
Drowned Compact elder's hall — Vaela Fenwall's space
goldwater_guaranteed: Wine production facilities x6 — family operations

amber_coast_food_production.description: Unlike Keth, the Amber Coast produces significant food — fish, olive oil, wine, some grain. More food-secure but not self-sufficient for grain and luxury goods.
amber_coast_food_production.drought_impact: Drought years in Scaldas reduce olive oil and wine production significantly. Fishing is unaffected by drought but affected by storm seasons.
amber_coast_food_production.current_status: Adequate across all categories. The Salthaven fish supply is currently under informal quarantine by the fishing community elder — this is reducing fish availability without a public announcement, which is causing quiet price increases that will become visible within a week.

amber_coast_water.description: More reliable than Keth. City wells plus Cathedral Quarter rain catchment. Drought years reduce catchment.

goldwater_production.olive_oil: Premium quality, exported across Arneth. Current season's production adequate.
goldwater_production.wine: Premium quality. The price suppression arrangement has been keeping Goldwater producers below fair market value for three years. The documentation exists. The Ledger has not been engaged.

disease_quarantine_effect.fish_supply: The fishing community elder's informal quarantine of the specific supply chain is currently reducing Salthaven's fish market by approximately 30%. Velmonde fish prices will rise within one week if the quarantine continues. The Factorate does not know the quarantine is in effect.
disease_quarantine_effect.player_impact: A player attempting to buy fresh fish in Velmonde within a week of game start will encounter price increases and the explanation from vendors that 'the Salthaven boats aren't running their usual routes.' This is a thread.

unconscious_effect_on_amber_coast.source: Three Salthaven disease patients are experiencing the directional awareness — 'knowing which way is wrong instead of which way is north'
unconscious_effect_on_amber_coast.discovery: The Drowned Compact elder Vaela Fenwall has recorded this in oral records as unusual. She has not connected it to anything.
unconscious_effect_on_amber_coast.the_fish_boat: The boat that brought the infection — its origin before Salthaven is unclear. Tidewatch has its records. The Salt Silence's Tidewatch contact logged it. A player who traces the fish boat's origin will find it came from the southern Ossrath coast — the same area where the Keth Plateau's New Work site is located.
unconscious_effect_on_amber_coast.player_connection: A player who has experienced the directional awareness from the Thornsveld Marches disc, recognized the same phenomenon in Keth's Archive, and now encounters it in Salthaven's sick patients has the strongest possible evidence of the cross-regional artifact connection. The fish boat's Ossrath origin adds a third geographic data point to the same source.
